title: Antreprenor pe banii tăi: statul român vrea să producă apa minerală „Dacia”
summary: The Romanian government announced plans to launch a state-produced mineral water brand called 'Dacia,' entering an already mature and competitive market. The article critiques this as toxic state interventionism, arguing the state lacks the competence to compete with established private brands and will waste taxpayer money on a predictable failure, similar to past failed state ventures like the Unirea store.
talking_points: The Romanian mineral water market is worth approximately 1 billion euros with 80% controlled by five major brands, leaving little room for a state entrant
The state would be financing its product with taxes collected from the very private companies it seeks to compete against, creating a fundamental market distortion
Historical precedents of state entrepreneurial ventures in Romania have consistently failed, including the Unirea retail store and 83 state companies losing approximately 3 billion euros
Without proper distribution networks, the water would likely end up only in ministry protocols and CFR train cars, a niche too small to justify tens of millions in investment
Romania's role should be collecting fair royalties on its rich mineral water resources, not distorting the market through direct intervention
author: Adina Săniuță
